summary: Pakistani organizations should prioritize patching critical vulnerabilities, particularly CVE-2026-58644 and CVE-2026-25089, which are being actively exploited and have been added to the CISA KEV catalog, affecting multiple vendors including Microsoft and Google. The Pakistan CERT/NCCS has also issued alerts for PKCERT-BA33D102 and PKCERT-6AEF826A, which target local businesses and individuals, emphasizing the need for heightened vigilance against phishing and social engineering attacks. To mitigate these threats, organizations should immediately apply patches for CVE-2026-39808, CVE-2026-46817, and CVE-2023-4346, and ensure all systems and software are up-to-date, especially those from vendors like Adobe and Adobe Acrobat, which are commonly used in Pakistani enterprises.
